title: Decide: seed data, fix ownership, or adjust async
prompt: A test fails with `Ecto.NoResultsError` from `Accounts.get_user_by_email!("alice@example.com")`. The previous developer proposed three fixes: (A) `Repo.insert!(%User{email: "alice@example.com"})` in setup, (B) wrap the failing line in `Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.allow/3`, (C) mark the module `async: false`. The test's own body does `Accounts.create_user(%{email: "alice@example.com"})` before the failing assertion, and the create_user function performs an `Ecto.Changeset.validate_format/3` on email that rejects strings containing '@' if they also contain a space (a bug). Write `docs/diagnosis.md` identifying the correct fix: none of A, B, or C — the root cause is the validation bug in `create_user`. Must state that explicitly and must NOT endorse A, B, or C.
tier_rationale: Forces the agent past three tempting-looking anti-fixes to identify the real root cause in the insert path. Direct Claude failure mode.
research_citation: "A test fails. Claude queries the database. Finds nothing. Thinks there's a data problem." — BoothIQ
